zh.lifeSummary: 當Edward Beauchamp Seymour於1586年6月12日出生在英國米德萊可堂爾 本 倫敦時，他的父親Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp24歲，母親Honora Rogers24歲。他於1609年6月1日與Lady Ann Sackville在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 布利德也斯 邱奇 福利特 史崔特結婚。他去世於1618年9月15日在英國英格蘭維爾爾享年32歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 布利德也斯 邱奇 福利特 史崔特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edward Beauchamp Seymour于1586年6月12日出生在英国米德萊可堂爾 本 倫敦时，他的父亲Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp24岁，母亲Honora Rogers24岁。他于1609年6月1日与Lady Ann Sackville在英国英格兰倫敦聖 布利德也斯 邱奇 福利特 史崔特结婚。他去世于1618年9月15日在英国英格兰維爾爾享年32岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦聖 布利德也斯 邱奇 福利特 史崔特。

nameMeanings.name: Seymour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London): of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), and possibly also from Saint-Maur-des-Bois (Manche) and Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine), named from the dedication of their churches to Saint Maur (see   Moore   3). The family of this name who have held the title Duke of Somerset since 1547 are said by some to have originated in Saint-Maur-des-Fossés (Seine), while others claim they are from Saint-Maur-sur-Loire (Touraine). They rose to prominence under the Tudors, when Edward Seymour (died 1552) held a high position of office under Henry VIII, who was married to Edward's sister, Jane. The Norman name has been established in Ireland since at least the 17th century.

variants.name: Moore
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Seymore
meanings: English: variant of   Seymour  .

variants.name: Semar
meanings: Germanized form of French Saint Marc, the name of a Huguenot family that migrated to the Palatinate.
English: variant of   Seymour  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eudard

variants.name: Eduardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Eideard
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edward .

variants.name: Édouard
meanings: From English. See Edward .

variants.name: Edvard
meanings: form of English Edward .

variants.name: Eduard

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Transferred use of the surname derived from Gaelic cam beul ‘crooked mouth’. It is borne by one of the great Highland clans, whose head is the Duke of Argyll.

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Beauchamp Seymour was born on 12 June 1586, in Tower of London, Middlesex, England, his father, Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp, was 24 and his mother, Honora Rogers, was 24. He married Lady Ann Sackville on 1 June 1609, in St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, England, United Kingdom. He died on 15 September 1618, in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 32, and was buried in St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edward Beauchamp Seymour est né le 12 juin 1586, à Tower of London, Middlesex, Angleterre, son père, Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Honora Rogers, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Lady Ann Sackville le 1 juin 1609, à St Bride's Church Fleet Street, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 15 septembre 1618, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterré à St Bride's Church Fleet Street, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edward Beauchamp Seymour am 12. Juni 1586 in Tower of London, Middlesex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp 24 und seine Mutter Honora Rogers 24. Er hat am 1. Juni 1609 in St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, England, United Kingdom Lady Ann Sackville geheiratet. Er starb am 15. September 1618 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Beauchamp Seymour è nato il il 12 giugno 1586 nel Tower of London, Middlesex, England, il suo padre, Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Honora Rogers, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Lady Ann Sackville il 1 giugno 1609, nel St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 15 settembre 1618, nel Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolto a St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Edward Beauchamp Seymourが1586年6月12日にイギリスミッドレセックストウエル オフ ロンドンで生まれた時，父Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchampは24歳，母Honora Rogersは24歳であった。1609年6月1日にイギリスイギリスロンドンスト ブリデ'ス チャーチ フリート ストレエトでLady Ann Sackvilleと結婚した。彼は1618年9月15日にイギリスイギリスウィルトシュアイアで32歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスロンドンスト ブリデ'ス チャーチ フリート ストレエトにて埋葬された。

ko.St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London,: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 브라이데'스 c후르치 플릿 스트릿
ko.St Bride Churchyard, Fleet Street, City: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 브라이데'스 c후르치 플릿 스트릿
ko.lifeSummary: Edward Beauchamp Seymour(이)가 1586년 6월 12일 영국 미들레세스 타워 프 런던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Honora Rogers(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1609년 6월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 브라이데'스 c후르치 플릿 스트릿에서 Lady Ann Sackville(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1618년 9월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 브라이데'스 c후르치 플릿 스트릿에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edward Beauchamp Seymour nasceu em 12 de junho de 1586, em Tower of London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Honora Rogers, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Lady Ann Sackville em 1 de junho de 1609, em St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 15 de setembro de 1618, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 32 anos, e foi sepultado em St Bride's Church Fleet Street, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edward Beauchamp Seymour родился 12 июня 1586 г. в местности тоуир оф лондон, миддлесекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Edward Seymour Viscount Beauchamp было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Honora Rogers, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Ann Sackville 1 июня 1609 г. в местности ст бриде'с чурч флит стрит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 15 сентября 1618 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет и был похоронен в местности ст бриде'с чурч флит стрит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
